audits.excerpt: Part of her still reached for the same smaller job: secure the road out, count the dead, send a cleaner report home, pretend borders could still hold it.
audits.reason: This turns Sylvanas's decision pressure into summarized process thought. The scene already has the map case, horses, smoke, and page under hand; it should stay in those props instead of stepping out into mission-statement abstraction.
audits.suggestedFix: Sylvanas's thumb found the map case strap again. Road out. Dead counted. One cleaner dispatch home. The old border job still offered itself in pieces she could carry. North still sat on the page.

audits.excerpt: Vereesa Windrunner, dust to the knees, one glove gone, braid coming apart, riding like the Convocation had reached into the family and chosen the hand most likely to hurt.
audits.reason: The simile names the tactic instead of letting the horse, dispatch tube, and braced posture carry it. This is exactly the kind of portable explanatory line the grounding pass is supposed to cut back.
audits.suggestedFix: Vereesa Windrunner, dust to the knees, one glove gone, braid coming apart, the dispatch tube knocking her hip and her mouth already braced around the title she had come to use.

audits.excerpt: Jaina at Sylvanas's right shoulder with enough sense not to come nearer, the map case braced upright against her leg like a second witness.
audits.reason: "Like a second witness" is a neat line, but it lifts the moment into metaphor when the camp is already physically legible. The beat wants leather, wind, stance, and distance, not courtroom color.
audits.suggestedFix: Jaina at Sylvanas's right shoulder with enough sense not to come nearer, the map case braced upright against her leg, its leather edge tapping her boot whenever the wind caught it.

audits.excerpt: "Lady Sun. The family is not a relay post you can abandon because the road beyond it looks interesting."
audits.reason: "Interesting" softens the family wound into airy phrasing and undercuts the severity Vereesa is carrying into camp. The line should stay as field-language hurt, not half-wry abstraction.
audits.suggestedFix: "Lady Sun. The family is not a relay post you leave manned while you ride after the next fire."

audits.excerpt: "I think this is family speaking with councillors' hands on its throat."
audits.reason: The metaphor is portable and rhetorically clean, but too composed for the pressure already embodied by the seal, the vellum, and Vereesa's road-spent body. The refusal lands harder if Sylvanas answers through the actual instrument used on her sister.
audits.suggestedFix: "I think they put the seal in your hand because they knew whose voice would cut deepest."

audits.excerpt: She stood straight as a nailed board.
audits.reason: This is a stock simile in the most delicate POV-limited observation beat of the chapter. The stone scene is strongest when posture reads through exact bodily stillness and light behavior, not generic comparison language.
audits.suggestedFix: She stood so still the stone-light only moved where her pulse touched her wrist.
